title: Print a Phone Stand
description: Practice 3D printing by making a handy phone stand.

dialogue.text: Hey, Sydney again! Ready for a tidy phone stand? We'll tune the printer, slice a simple stand, and print it in white PLA so the details pop and the surface stays smooth.

Start with safety: ventilate the room, tie back hair, and keep sleeves clear of the gantry. Heat the nozzle to PLA temps, wipe it clean, and paper-level the bed so you feel light drag at all four corners. Clean the build plate with isopropyl to help first-layer adhesion, then purge to white PLA until the filament comes out consistent and bubble-free.

options.text: Swap to white PLA and purge.

dialogue.text: Open the Phone Stand STL from your community library inventory, then slice it with a 0.2 mm layer height, 3 perimeters, 5 top/bottom layers, and 15% gyroid infill. Orient the stand flat so the phone rests on solid perimeters; supports should stay off unless your slicer shows a true overhang. Enable a 3-5 mm brim if your printer struggles with adhesion, and if the first layer won't stick, re-clean the plate, increase the brim, or nudge the Z-offset down a touch. Use ~200°C nozzle and 60°C bed for PLA, then watch the first layer for full contact. Let the print finish undisturbed, cool completely, and flex the bed to release without prying.

options.text: The stand is finished and cooled!

dialogue.text: Nice work! Test-fit your phone: the back should lean 60–70° and the lip should hold the phone without blocking the charging port. If it feels tight, sand the lip lightly; if it feels loose, scale the model up 1-2% and reprint. Power down the printer and log your settings for the next run.
